title: Creation and processing of different sample types for Whole Genome Sequencing of Vibrio cholerae
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fch81","text":"This protocol describes laboratory procedures for the creation and preparation of alternate sample types for collection during a cholera outbreak for whole genome sequencing. The purpose of this protocol is to evaluate the implementation of low cost and easy-to-use methods of sample collection and preservation to generate usable sequences in cholera surveillance.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4aq6k","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f7tpp","text":"Whole stool and samples enriched by alkaline peptone water were collected in an outbreak setting according to standard operating procedures and transported to our laboratory at Johns Hopkins University. Additional samples were created from whole stool in a controlled laboratory setting. DNA is extracted from the matched samples in parallel according to the procedures described in this protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d876q","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"actr5","text":"This protocol was developed to address the high costs associated with the gold standard sample method for whole genome sequencing, which is isolation of V. cholerae through culture. Laboratory resources for culture and cold chain storage are limited in the countries that experience cholera epidemics. Evaluation of culture-independent methods of sample collection and transportation could provide more cost-effective ways of specimen handling.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
creator: Audrey Safir
